category_title: At-risk four-year-old students exhibiting varied oral language behaviors
minimal_context: The student is playing in the block center with peers and communicating about their building activity.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: It is time for center play. What are you doing in the block center?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (To peer) I am going to build a castle in the block center. (To tutor) Castle.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What is going to go inside the castle?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Big dogs. (To peer) If you don’t give me that toy, you’ll be sorry!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: It is time for center play. What are you doing in the block center?

STUDENT: (To peer) I am going to build a castle in the block center. (To tutor) Castle.

TUTOR: What is going to go inside the castle?

STUDENT: Dogs.

TUTOR: Tell me more about that.

STUDENT: Big dogs. (To peer) If you don’t give me that toy, you’ll be sorry!

task.content: Child selected learning experiences in which children choose the direction of the experience
task.learning_goal: Use expressive language to convey a thought, want, or need

context.social_setting: Non-teaching directed experiences, communicating with peers and adults

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When speaking to adults during non-teaching directed experiences
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Used one word responses 28.8% of the time, phrases 15.4% of the time

behavior_patterns.condition: When trying to get ones way
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Uses aggressive/bullying language defined as threatening words
related_quotes: If you don’t give me that toy, you’ll be sorry!
I want this book. You can get one off the shelf.

behavior_patterns.condition: When communicating with peers compared to adults
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Demonstrated significantly more desired language usage when communicating with peers
related_quotes: I am going to build a castle in the block center.
